title: Aira curtifolia
intro: Aira curtiseta, also known as Aira, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which provide relief and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Aira curtiseta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Aira curtiseta is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion in herbal medicine, and is sometimes used to make teas, tinctures, and salves for topical application.
taxonomy: Aira curtifolia is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Poeae and the genus Aira. Aira curtifolia is further classified as a member of the subfamily Pooideae and the order Poales. The plant is native to various parts of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and distribution patterns.

Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Aira curtifolia as a medicinal herb.
